# @julianobazzi/nextjs-utils

> A collection of reusable hooks and utilities for Next.js (App Router), written
> in TypeScript. Built for Next.js, compatible with React 18 and 19. Client hooks
> ship with the `'use client'` directive applied at the bundle entry, so they can be
> imported directly into Server Components. `next`, `react`, `react-dom` are peer
> dependencies. Ships as ESM + CJS with TypeScript declarations.

Two entry points:
- Root `@julianobazzi/nextjs-utils` — core React hooks + utilities (no `next/navigation`).
- Subpath `@julianobazzi/nextjs-utils/next` — the four `next/navigation`-backed hooks
  (`useSearchParam`, `useUpdateQueryParams`, `useUpdateSearchParam`, `useFilterQueryParams`).
Keeping `next/navigation` out of the root entry lets server-evaluated modules (e.g.
`next build` page-data collection) import core hooks without an `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.
`import { useToggle } from '@julianobazzi/nextjs-utils'`
`import { useSearchParam } from '@julianobazzi/nextjs-utils/next'`

## Hooks

- useToggle(initialValue = false): [value: boolean, toggle: () => void, set: (value?: boolean) => void]
  — Boolean state. `toggle()` flips it; `set(v)` assigns a value, `set()` flips.

- usePrevious<T>(value: T): T | undefined
  — Returns the value held on the previous render; `undefined` on first render.

- useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay = 500): T
- useDebounce<F extends (...args: any[]) => any>(fn: F, delay = 500): (...args: Parameters<F>) => void
  — Overloaded. Value form returns a debounced copy of `value` that updates after
    `delay` ms of stillness. Function form returns a stable debounced callback that
    runs the latest `fn` after `delay` ms of inactivity (cancelled on unmount).

- useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T): [value: T, setValue: (v: T | ((prev: T) => T)) => void, remove: () => void]
  — State persisted in localStorage, JSON-serialized, synced across components and
    tabs via the `storage` event. SSR-safe (returns `initialValue` without `window`).

- useMediaQuery(query: string): boolean
  — Reactively tracks a CSS media query (e.g. '(min-width: 768px)') via
    window.matchMedia and useSyncExternalStore. Returns `false` on the server.

- useEventListener(eventName, handler, element?): void
  — Attaches a typed event listener to window (default), a DOM element, or a ref,
    with automatic cleanup. The handler is stored in a ref, so it need not be stable.

- useInterval(callback: () => void, delay: number | null): void
  — Runs `callback` every `delay` ms using the latest callback without resetting
    the timer. Pass `delay = null` to pause; clears on unmount.

- createCan<T>(useCurrentRoles: () => T | T[] | null | undefined): { useCan, Can }
  — Auth-agnostic role-based access control factory. `useCan(allowed?: T[]): boolean`
    and `Can({ allowed?, children, fallback? })` share the resolver. Unauthenticated
    (resolver -> null/undefined) is denied; empty/omitted `allowed` grants any
    authenticated user; otherwise granted if any current role is in `allowed`.

## Utilities (non-hook, src/utils/)

- allowNumericKeyDown(event: KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>, options?: { allowDecimal?: boolean; allowNegative?: boolean }): void
  — onKeyDown handler restricting an input to digits via preventDefault. Control,
    navigation, and modifier-shortcut keys pass through. Options allow '.'/',' and '-'.

## Next.js utilities (src/next/)

- useSearchParam(key: string, defaultValue?: string): string | null
  — Typed wrapper around next/navigation's useSearchParams; reads one query param.
    With a defaultValue returns string; otherwise string | null. Client-only.

- useUpdateQueryParams<T>(options?: { ignoredParams?, history?: 'push'|'replace', scroll? }): (params?: Partial<T>) => void
  — Writes multiple URL search params from an object. Empty values ('', null,
    undefined, []) delete the key; arrays append repeated entries; no argument
    clears all params. Default history 'replace', scroll false. ignoredParams
    defaults to [] (no app-specific defaults).

- useUpdateSearchParam(options?: { key?='search', history?, scroll? }): (value?: string) => void
  — Convenience over useUpdateQueryParams for one param. Empty value removes it;
    a value equal to the current URL value is a no-op.

- useFilterQueryParams<T>({ parameters, deps, search?, setSearch?, initialSearch?, ignoredParams?, overrides? }): { handleSearch }
  — Syncs a filter form's state to the URL: re-syncs on [deps, search], seeds the
    search term from initialSearch, returns a handleSearch(value?) setter.

## Notes

- Peer deps: next (>=14), react, react-dom (>=18 <20). The library never bundles them.
- Targets the App Router; client hooks carry the 'use client' directive.
- Build: tsup. Lint/format: Biome. Tests: Vitest + Testing Library (jsdom).
- Source layout: src/hooks/ (React hooks), src/utils/ (pure non-hook helpers), src/next/ (Next-specific).
- Source: https://github.com/julianobazzi/nextjs-utils
- Full docs: ./README.md (English) and ./README.pt-br.md (Portuguese).
